rulings.Case Title: ANDREA SPARROCK VS. PAULINE CONWAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Full And Complete Responses To Supplemental Discovery Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 31, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT PAULINE CONWAY'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Full And Complete Responses To Supplemental Discovery Set Two. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Conway's unopposed motion is granted. Plaintiff shall serve complete responses to all of the supplemental discovery by September 14. 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT CHIEF ASIAN, LLC's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant Chief Asian, LLC's motion to vacate default and default judgment is GRANTED. No substantive opposition filed. Counsel Kutrubes demonstrates good cause for relief under the mandatory provisions of CCP 473. Kutrubes shall remit $750 to plaintiff by September 28, 2018 as reasonable fees and costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: BRETT TODOROFF VS. JOE QUINN BRETZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants Joe Quinn Bretz And Randolph Bretz For Relief From Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 31, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT JOE BRETZ, RANDOLPH BRETZ's Motion By Defendants Joe Quinn Bretz And Randolph Bretz For Relief From Judgment. Defendants Joe Bretz and Randolph Bretz's motion for relief from judgment under CCP §473(b) is GRANTED. California law favors trials on the merits, and strong public policy favors granting the relief provided by CCP §473(b) when parties move for relief within six months of the judgment. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 981-82.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment and must bring the proposed order and judgment to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: BAUER INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC. VS. ONTRACK EVENT MANAGEMENT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Discovery - Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With April 30, 2018 Court Order; Request For Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,460 Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, August 31, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANTS ONTRACK EVENT MANAGEMENT, INC., JIMMY ENGELMAN'S Motion To Compel Compliance With April 30, 2018 Court Order; Request For Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,460 Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel is denied. The Reservation Listing By Sales Rep reports do not fall within the document requests to which the court previously granted a motion to compel, so the court's order granting that motion wasn't violated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
